content: UNIT 1 Basic Motor Movements Welcome to the exciting world of movement! As we have already learnt basic motor movements in Grade 3, we use our big muscles for actions like jumping, throwing, catching, kicking, and hitting. These are called gross motor skills and are important for our daily activities, playing sports, and staying fit. Fine motor skills, on the other hand, use the small muscles in our hands and fingers for delicate tasks like writing, drawing, picking up tiny objects, and gripping. Both gross and fine motor skills are part of basic motor movements. There are five main movements we focus on: throwing, catching, kicking, receiving, and striking. Our goal is to use these movements in fun activities with the help of objects of different shapes, sizes, weights, and textures.
learning_objectives: Understand gross motor skills and their importance
Recognize fine motor skills and their applications
Identify five main movements in basic motor skills

content: Throwing and catching are basic motor movements that require coordination, balance, and focus.
learning_objectives: Understand the key components of a good throwing technique
Recognize how a proper stance contributes to effective catching
Explain why focusing on the target is important when throwing or catching

topic: Catching a Ball Over Your Head
content: Stand with knees slightly bent, ready to jump, and position your hands above your head to form a'reverse cup'. Keep your head up and eyes on the ball, and pull your elbows towards your body while holding the ball firmly.
learning_objectives: Stand with knees slightly bent and body behind the ball.
Form a'reverse cup' with your hands above your head.
Keep your head up and eyes on the ball, and bend your elbows towards your body.

content: Form multiple teams with five students each and stand in a straight line facing each other s back at a distance of one metre. First student has the ball and on the blow of the whistle, the student will throw the ball over his head to the student behind him.
learning_objectives: Adjust force based on ball size and weight
Coordinate between the thrower and the catcher
Practice is essential for mastery

content: Form multiple teams with ten students each with a designated leader standing in the middle with a ball.
learning_objectives: Develop throwing and catching skills
Enhance reaction time
Observe and maintain balance

content: Develop a sense of space, distance, and accuracy.

Appreciate the significance of teamwork and communication in group activities.

content: The force required to throw a ball into a bucket accurately depends on the distance between the thrower and the bucket. The type of ball used can affect the ease of throwing and catching. Utilizing waste materials like paper and clothes can be a creative way to make playing equipment.
learning_objectives: Understand the impact of distance on throwing force
Recognize how ball type affects gameplay
Appreciate the use of waste materials in creating play equipment

content: The objective is to score a goal in a designated area using only direct or single-bounce passes.

content: Circle Time is a game that helps develop quick thinking and decision-making. It also improves hand-eye coordination and balance.

content: In this activity, students learn to pass and catch a ball using their feet.

content: Kicking and receiving are fundamental motor skills in sports. Kicking involves striking a ball with the foot, while receiving is about catching or stopping a ball.
learning_objectives: Proper stance and approach are crucial for effective kicking.
Body positioning and leg coordination are key for successful receiving.

content: Balance your body as the ball touches the ground.

Learn the technique of receiving the ball with the instep and following through.

content: Dribbling a medium-sized ball while walking and then kicking it to a partner to develop force and leg-eye coordination with anticipation.
learning_objectives: Maintain control and coordination through dribbling while walking.
Develop anticipation and force when kicking the ball to a partner.
Enhance learning experience by varying the number of students and balls in a group.

topic: Developing Leg-Eye Coordination and Partner Coordination
content: Line up students ten meters apart, place two cones in the middle forming a door, instruct students to kick the ball between the cones, increase or decrease distance based on skill levels, use cones and large-sized balls, vary the size of the door, use alternate legs, vary the size of the ball
learning_objectives: To develop leg-eye coordination

content: Circle Time is a game that helps students develop focus, concentration, and precision.

content: Develop anticipation, precision, and adaptability through zig-zag kicking with or without a defender.
learning_objectives: Develop ability to anticipate movement and space
Improve precise coordination and timing
Enhance adaptability and strategic thinking

content: Reprint 2026-27 Khel Yoga| Grade 4 30 BM 12 Foot Baseball Required Material Football, cones How to play Form teams according to the number of students. Mark four cones in a square shape, 15 20 metres apart. Cones will be treated as bases. Place a cone at the centre of the square for the pitcher to roll the ball. One team is the defence team and the other is the offence team. The pitcher of the defence team will roll the ball, and one student from the offence team will kick the ball outside of the square aerially. If the defence team catches an aerial pass, then the kicker will be out and if the ball is not caught or goes rolling on the ground, then the kicker will run to the second base and so on. A new kicker will come to kick and the game goes on. If a student fails to kick the ball three times, then they would be out. If three students get out, then the other team will play offence and the first team will defend.
learning_objectives: Understand the roles of the offense and defense teams
Recognize the importance of marking bases with cones
Identify the conditions under which a player is considered out

topic: Basic Motor Movements and Racquet Sports
content: To foster teamwork and spirit, adjust the playing area size based on the number of students, change the ball size accordingly, engage in Circle Time, and every student gets a turn to kick the ball; the team with the most points wins.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of teamwork in motor movements
Learn how striking an object with a racquet enhances learning
Describe a scenario where striking the shuttlecock with a racquet is particularly effective

topic: Proper Technique for Striking and Receiving with a Racquet in Badminton
content: Adopt a sideways stance with legs shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and eyes focused on the shuttlecock; hold the racquet with a dominant hand, forming a V shape between the thumb and index finger; execute a forward step, rotate hips and shoulders, maintain eye contact with the shuttlecock, and extend arms fully at the point of contact; the follow-through is crucial as it ensures the racquet's upper body swings along with it, finishing over the shoulder of the non-dominant hand, maximizing power and accuracy
learning_objectives: Maintain a sideways stance for stability and focus
Form a V grip with the racquet for control and power
Execute a full follow-through to maximize stroke effectiveness

content: Maintain a stable stance with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and body facing the shuttlecock. Grip the racquet gently with the dominant hand. Anticipate the shuttlecock's path, keep the body low, and align accordingly. Hit the shuttlecock back underhand or overhand based on its height. Ensure the racquet connects with the shuttlecock.
learning_objectives: Maintain a stable stance for better balance and reaction time
Grip the racquet gently with the dominant hand
Anticipate the shuttlecock's path to position optimally
Choose between underhand and overhand shots based on shuttlecock height
Ensure proper racquet connection for effective play

content: Develop collaborative and communication skills with coordination; Focus on basic motor movements; Simple language on how to play Circle Time and Star and Wish

content: If the raider laughs, smiles, or changes facial expressions, the opponent will get a point and the raider will be out. The raider scores a point by maintaining a straight face until the end. The team with the maximum number of points will win. Variations vary the time limit for each raid.

content: This game is played in different parts of the country and in fairs and festivals.
learning_objectives: The main objective of the game is to bring all students of the team opponent to their side.

A student contributes to their team's success by anticipating where their teammates will throw the ball and quickly deciding whether to catch it and run back to their side or to disrupt the opponent's team.
Skills developed through this game include agility, anticipation, and teamwork.

topic: Divide the students into pairs based on body weight and height and play a game to improve physical strength and grip, promote teamwork and fair play, and foster love and compassion towards nature.
content: Ask them to sit face-to-face by touching each other's feet without bending their knees as shown in the figure below. The students will hold a stick in such a manner that it is directly above their feet. On the blow of the whistle, students will start pulling the stick until a student falls or leaves the stick. The teacher must ensure that the students do not pull the stick before the whistle and the start is fair. Vary the stick with a ball to make it difficult to hold. Discuss what helped you to hold the stick and not give up even when you were tired. Reprint 2026-27 Khel Yoga| Grade 4 70 OG 13 Turai Kar This game is played in the Himalayan Region. Turai Kar means do your best and it encourages love and compassion towards nature.
learning_objectives: To improve the strength and grip of the students
To encourage teamwork and fair play

topic: Protecting the Environment Through Team Games

content: The first limb of Ashtanga Yoga is Yama, which consists of five principles: Ahimsa, Satya, Asteya, Brahmacharya, and Aparigraha.

content: Satya, or truthfulness, is the practice of being honest in one's thoughts, speech, and actions. Truthfulness builds trust and integrity, which are foundational to healthy relationships and a harmonious society. Practicing Satya helps in personal growth, as it encourages self-awareness and authenticity.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of truthfulness in building trust and fostering healthy relationships.
Recognize how practicing Satya contributes to personal integrity and growth.
Identify situations where being truthful might be challenging.

content: The woodcutter chose to tell the truth because he believed in honesty and integrity. He felt that keeping the axe would be dishonest and wrong.
learning_objectives: Honesty is a valuable trait that builds trust and respect.
Keeping something that doesn't belong to you is considered theft.
The woodcutter's honesty was rewarded by the Goddess.

content: Santosha is the practice of finding contentment and satisfaction in what we have, rather than constantly desiring more.
learning_objectives: Understand that Santosha is about appreciating and valuing what we have.
Recognize that Santosha teaches us to find happiness in our current circumstances.
Identify Santosha as a key practice in Ashtanga Yoga for personal growth.
Comprehend that Santosha shifts focus from external desires to internal contentment.
Apply Santosha by reflecting on a time of contentment and writing about its application in daily life.
Avoid confusing contentment with complacency and the belief that contentment means never wanting more.

content: The story illustrates the concept of desire and the consequences of wishing for more power or status.
learning_objectives: Understand how desires can lead to a cycle of transformation with unforeseen consequences.
Recognize the importance of humility and contentment.
Identify the challenges and limitations associated with every desire.

topic: Finding Happiness in Oneself and the Present
content: Contentment is about appreciating what we have, happiness can be found in simple things and the people around us, it's important to recognize and value our own strengths and abilities
learning_objectives: List five things you are grateful for today
Reflect on a moment when you felt truly happy and write about it

content: Have you seen singers preparing themselves before singing a song? Similarly, preparatory exercises help loosen our joints and prepare our bodies making it easier and safer to perform yoga poses or asanas.

topic: Yoga Exercises for Muscular Strength and Balance

content: Stand straight with a little gap between your legs, bring hands in front of chest, inhale and stretch arms horizontally, exhale and bring hands back, repeat five times; inhale and stretch arms vertically, exhale and bring hands back, repeat five times; inhale and stretch arms diagonally, exhale and bring hands back, repeat five times.
learning_objectives: Perform hand stretch in three ways: horizontal, oblique, and vertical
Understand that each stretch targets different muscle groups and improves flexibility
Recognize the importance of proper form in performing the hand stretch

content: Asana is the third limb of Ashtanga Yoga involving body postures to maintain strength, flexibility, and health.
learning_objectives: Asanas require stability and comfort.
Types include standing, sitting, balancing, twisting, supine, and prone asanas.
Guidelines for practice involve empty stomach, non-slippery surface, slow performance, and informing teachers about discomfort.

content: This section explores the practice of Vishranti, a yoga posture resembling a half-wheel.
learning_objectives: Understand that Vishranti is a yoga posture resembling a half-wheel.
Know that the posture involves inhaling, bending backward with straight knees, and exhaling to relax.
Recognize the importance of keeping the knees and back straight during the posture.
Understand how Vishranti engages core muscles and stretches the spine.
Identify the benefits of practicing Vishranti, including improved flexibility, reduced stress, and enhanced concentration.
Practice Vishranti by following the steps outlined in the chapter.
Perform Vishranti for 5-10 minutes daily to improve spinal flexibility and reduce stress.
Avoid common mistakes such as bending the knees inward or rounding the back.
Maintain hands beside the feet in the final position.
Recall that Vishranti helps improve spinal flexibility and reduce stress.

content: Padahastasana, Trikonasana, and Shashankasana are yoga postures with specific benefits.
learning_objectives: Understand the core concept of Padahastasana and its benefits.

content: Inhale, slowly come up, place hands on hips, bend backwards (Ardha Ushtrasana), hold for a few seconds, stretch legs forward and relax in Shithila Dandasana.
learning_objectives: Begin in Dandasana.
Transition to Vajrasana.
Perform Ardha Ushtrasana.
Hold Ardha Ushtrasana for a few seconds.
Stretch legs forward and relax in Shithila Dandasana.

content: This yoga posture strengthens the spine and improves flexibility in the lower back.
learning_objectives: Setubandhasana, also known as Bridge Pose, engages the muscles of the back, shoulders, and legs.
The final posture of Setubandhasana requires the chin to touch the chest, which helps in opening the throat and improving respiratory function.
Setubandhasana is a part of the Khel Yoga series, specifically designed for Grade 4 students.

content: Uttanpadasana is a yoga posture where the practitioner lies on their back and lifts the legs upwards while keeping the knees straight.

content: Begin by placing your hands below your thighs with palms facing downwards, and rest your chin on the ground. Inhale and raise both legs, keeping the knees straight. Exhale slowly, bring the legs down, and release the hands.

content: Anuloma Viloma Pranayama and Brahmari Pranayama are two yoga breathing techniques that help in controlling the breath and calming the mind.

content: Hasta Mudras are specific hand gestures used in Pranayama and Dhyana to focus the mind and enhance concentration. Each Mudra has a unique benefit. Karnarandra Dhauti cleans the ear canals, while Kapalarandra Dhauti cleanses the nasal passages.

content: The content discusses two specific practices for maintaining ear hygiene and improving concentration.
learning_objectives: Practice ear cleaning method daily.
Perform Kapalarandra Dhauti during morning bathing.
Enhance concentration through Krida Yoga Dharana.

topic: # Book: deky103.pdf ## Chapter: Unit 3 ### Section: Concept Block 79
content: The concept of focus and concentration in a distracting environment.

topic: The importance of a healthy diet and mindful eating habits
content: Eating fresh and healthy food is essential for maintaining good health. Avoiding junk food can prevent health issues. A balanced diet provides the necessary nutrients for the body to function properly. Consuming fruits and vegetables can boost the immune system. Avoiding junk food reduces the risk of obesity and related diseases. Eating mindfully allows one to enjoy the food and recognize when they are full, preventing overeating.
learning_objectives: To understand the benefits of eating fresh and healthy food
To recognize the health risks associated with junk food
To appreciate the role of a balanced diet in overall health
To learn how mindful eating enhances digestion and nutrient absorption

content: Establishing a consistent wake-up time helps regulate the body's internal clock. Regular practice of asana and pranayama contributes to physical and mental health. Helping at home and engaging in community activities foster a sense of responsibility and social skills.

content: The teacher will assess the students based on their politeness, helping others, sharing, watering plants, feeding animals.

content: Each group is given an incomplete story and is asked to complete the story after discussing in the group and share their views with the class.

Seek guidance for not very clear concepts
Avoid lack of creativity and structure in work

content: Pranayama is a yogic practice that involves breath control to enhance the flow of prana (life force) in the body.
learning_objectives: Pranayama is a key aspect of yoga that focuses on breathing techniques.
It helps in improving concentration, reducing stress, and promoting overall well-being.
Pranayama can be practiced in various forms, such as Ujjayi, Kapalabhati, and Anulom Vilom.
Pranayama works by regulating the breath, which in turn affects the nervous system.
The Ujjayi breath helps to calm the mind and focus the practitioner's attention.
Not maintaining a steady rhythm while practicing Pranayama is a common mistake.
Forgetting to focus on the breath and the mind is another common mistake.

content: The concept of a suggestive timetable in physical education emphasizes the importance of structured time allocation to achieve specific learning outcomes.
learning_objectives: Allocating block periods for Physical Education and Well-being is essential for comprehensive student development.
Different session types are designed to optimize the limited time in each period for various educational objectives.
A sample time allocation for a 40-minute duration is provided for all session types to guide teachers in planning.

topic: Session Planning and Khel Yoga Structure
content: Two types of sessions can be planned with block periods: Type 4 with a warm-up, gamified drills, cool-down, and circle time; and Type 5 with a warm-up, game time for two games, cool-down, and circle time.
learning_objectives: Understand the structure of Type 4 block period sessions.
Know the components of Type 5 block period sessions.
Learn the steps of a Khel Yoga session.

content: The opening and closing prayers in a Yoga session are essential for setting a respectful and spiritual tone.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of opening and closing prayers in a Yoga session.
Recognize how these prayers help in creating a mindful and respectful atmosphere.
Identify the key elements of the opening and closing prayers.

content: Begin a yoga session by sitting in a comfortable Sukhasana posture, ensuring a straight back, closed eyes, and a gentle smile. Place your hands in Chin Mudra and focus on your breath, then take a deep breath in and chant Om as you exhale. Transition to Namaskarah Mudra, chant the prayer, and observe any changes. Gently rub your palms together, shape them into a cup, and place them on your eyes.
learning_objectives: The Sukhasana posture is the foundation for a peaceful yoga session.
Chanting Om promotes inner peace and creates a spiritual connection.
The Namaskarah Mudra enhances concentration and is a gesture of respect.
Rubbing the palms together and placing them on the eyes symbolizes the transfer of energy.

content: Ashtanga Yoga is a comprehensive system of yoga that consists of eight limbs designed to guide practitioners towards a balanced and fulfilling life, both physically and spiritually.
learning_objectives: Ashtanga Yoga is not just a physical practice but a holistic approach to life.
The practice of Ashtanga Yoga aims to harmonize the body, mind, and spirit, leading to self-realization and a deeper understanding of the self and the universe.
The eight limbs of Ashtanga Yoga serve as a roadmap for living a meaningful life, emphasizing the importance of moral and ethical conduct, self-discipline, and spiritual growth.

topic: Structured Approach to Teaching Yoga and Pranayama
content: A systematic approach to teaching yoga and pranayama is essential for effective learning and skill development.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of a structured teaching approach in yoga and pranayama
Identify the role of preparatory practices in yoga
Recognize the benefits of regular assessment in a yoga class

content: The document outlines a structured approach to physical education, emphasizing the importance of both Our Games and Yoga in developing motor skills and personal goals.
learning_objectives: Each unit is designed with a variety of activities, including Our Games like Throwing and Catching, and Yoga practices such as Coordination and Krida Yoga.
The plan aims to enhance coordination, throwing, catching, and personal goal setting among students.
Summative assessments are included to evaluate students' progress and understanding of the skills and concepts taught.

content: Throwing and catching are fundamental motor skills that require practice, focus, and coordination.
learning_objectives: Proper technique is crucial for accuracy and power in throwing.
Coordination and focus are essential for effective catching.
Teamwork and communication can enhance throwing skills.

topic: Receiving Passes and Hitting Balls in Khel Yoga Grade 4
content: In Khel Yoga Grade 4, students learn to confidently receive passes from any direction and control them, and practice picking up balls after practice.
learning_objectives: Shift weight onto the non-receiving foot to receive a pass effectively
Maintain proper balance, use strong force, and accuracy to hit the ball
Demonstrate positive attitude even after losing a game

topic: Hitting a Ball and Jumping Techniques
content: To hit a ball, stand sideways with legs shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and eyes on the ball; step forward on the front foot, swing while rotating hips, and extend arms fully at the contact point; follow through by swinging the upper body with the apparatus finishing over the non-dominant shoulder; jumping requires balance, coordination, and leg strength; not keeping knees slightly bent can lead to a loss of balance; failing to extend arms fully at the contact point can reduce the power of the hit
learning_objectives: Understand the correct stance for hitting a ball
Learn how to follow through after making contact with the ball
Recognize the importance of keeping eyes on the ball
Identify common mistakes in hitting and jumping techniques
Practice hitting a ball with a partner focusing on stance and follow-through
Perform exercises focusing on balance and coordination for jumping

content: Guide students to observe the above points while performing the skills and/or assessing the skill for themselves.
learning_objectives: I can walk for 5 minutes without any discomfort.
I can walk for 7 minutes without any discomfort.
I can continuously jog for 3 minutes.
I can continuously jog for 5 minutes.
I enjoy running and walking.

content: Helping Others and Kindness is about offering assistance to others without being asked, with a genuine sense of empathy.
learning_objectives: Regularly offering help without being asked demonstrates a strong sense of empathy and a desire to support others in need.
Feeling good when being kind to others can motivate continuous acts of kindness, creating a positive cycle of support and respect.
Setting a positive example by treating everyone with respect and fairness encourages others to do the same, fostering a community of kindness.

content: Students are expected to take initiative in handling schoolwork and daily duties without frequent reminders.
learning_objectives: Students should be proactive in managing their schoolwork and daily responsibilities.
They must consistently fulfill their personal and shared duties.
Tasks should be organized and completed efficiently.

content: Consuming a balanced diet with fresh fruits, vegetables, and whole grains provides the body with necessary nutrients; avoiding junk food helps prevent health issues such as obesity, diabetes, and heart disease; eating with awareness, such as mindful eating, can improve digestion and satisfaction from meals.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of eating fresh and healthy food for good health
Identify the consequences of consuming junk food regularly
Recognize how eating with awareness benefits health

content: Establishing a consistent wake-up time helps regulate the body's internal clock. Regular practice of asana and pranayama contributes to physical and mental health. Helping at home and engaging in community activities foster a sense of responsibility and social skills.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of a consistent wake-up time.
Recognize the health benefits of asana and pranayama.
Appreciate the social and personal benefits of helping at home.

content: Students are evaluated on their behavior and actions, which reflect their character and values.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of speaking politely
Recognize the significance of helping others
Appreciate the value of sharing with friends

content: # Educational Material: deky103.pdf ## Chapter: Unknown ### Section: Concept Block 104
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of frequency (Always, Often, Sometimes, Rarely)
Identify key ideas related to the concept
Explain why the concept works with examples
Answer questions to check understanding
Complete exercises for practice
Avoid common mistakes
Revise key points
Observe behavioral criteria for assessment

content: Each group is given an incomplete story and is asked to complete the story after discussing in the group and share their views with the class.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate clear understanding and active participation
Perform all asanas correctly
Explain core concepts in simple language

content: Pranayama is a practice in yoga that involves breath control. It's a technique to enhance the flow of prana (life force) in the body, which can lead to improved health and spiritual growth.
learning_objectives: Identify the benefits of practicing Pranayama
Explain how Pranayama affects the nervous system
Describe a Pranayama technique
